claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit that detects tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects via git ls-files and filesystem walk fallback.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot combines git ls-files output with recursive filesystem walk and excludes allowed artifact roots.
evidence.why: Core boundary snapshot implementation that covers both git-backed and non-git projects.

evidence.summary: gitFiles runs git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z to get tracked, cached, and other untracked non-excluded files.
evidence.why: Git-backed file discovery covers tracked, cached, and untracked files.

evidence.summary: walkFiles recursively walks filesystem while excluding .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache directories.
evidence.why: Non-git fallback ensures coverage even without git or for files outside git index.

evidence.summary: Combines git files and walk files when git is available: git ? [...new Set([...git, ...walkFiles(projectRoot)])] : walkFiles(projectRoot).
evidence.why: Union of git-tracked and walked files ensures complete coverage for git-backed projects.

evidence.summary: isWithin checks if a path is within a root by resolving relative path and ensuring it does not start with .. and is not absolute.
evidence.why: Path containment logic used to exclude artifact roots from mutation detection.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after SHA256 digests and returns added, modified, and deleted file changes.
evidence.why: Change detection logic that identifies mutations between snapshots.

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshot captured before agent execution: const boundaryBefore = buildBoundarySnapshot(plan.project_root, [plan.output_dir]);
evidence.why: Pre-execution baseline allows detection of mutations during agent runs.

evidence.summary: Post-execution boundary snapshot compared and stored in result.boundary with project_mutation status.
evidence.why: Post-execution audit produces violation report for PRD readiness gating.

evidence.summary: Git-backed boundary test creates untracked-mutation.txt and verifies boundary violation with added change detection.
evidence.why: Test evidence that git untracked mutations are detected.

evidence.summary: Gitignored boundary test creates .env file outside excluded dirs and verifies violation even though gitignored.
evidence.why: Test evidence that gitignored files are still covered by boundary audit.

evidence.summary: Non-git boundary test modifies src/existing.txt, writes agent-scratch.txt artifact, and verifies only project file mutation is detected.
evidence.why: Test evidence that non-git projects use walkFiles fallback and correctly exclude allowed artifact roots.

evidence.summary: Agent runtime directory configuration passes plan.output_dir as runtimeDir for artifact writes.
evidence.why: Evidence that allowed artifact roots are derived from dispatch plan.

evidence.summary: Boundary mutations become BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers in readiness with explicit path and change details.
evidence.why: Evidence that boundary violations block PRD readiness.

risks: Boundary audit may miss mutations in non-standard excluded directories if walkFiles excludedDir list does not cover all cases.
Race condition possible if multiple agents write concurrently between before/after snapshots.
Files created and deleted within the same agent execution may not be detected if snapshot timing only captures endpoints.

result.notes: Boundary audit implementation is complete with git ls-files plus walkFiles fallback, correctly excludes artifact roots, and has test coverage for git-backed untracked, gitignored, and non-git scenarios. Acceptance criteria met.
